hardness_gate: flash outcome in {failed, interrupted} AND pro outcome == succeeded
repeat_action_rate_definition: fraction of consecutive same-tool actions whose click coords are within ~2% of screen width/height of each other, computed post-hoc from sessions_events.csv tool_args
execution_order: 1. Validate h/desktop environment with a single trivial probe task (not part of hardness pool)
2. Run all pool tasks once on h/web-surfer-flash, log via export_session_csv.py
3. Run h/web-surfer-pro only on tasks where flash failed/interrupted
4. Apply hardness_gate + rank by severity metrics
5. Select final stratified set (top-N, spanning categories) as locked hard-trajectory benchmark

desktop_probe_task.instruction: Open the file manager and rename one existing file to 'probe-ok'.
desktop_probe_task.purpose: Validate h/desktop environment provisions and responds at all before committing 4 pool slots to it.

readonly_desktop_pool.purpose: Non-destructive local-desktop counterparts to the osworld-* pool entries: same hardness taxonomy (nested menus, long-horizon composition, dynamic-UI gestures, ambiguous targets), but every task is read-only or ends in its original state, so nothing on this machine gets edited/saved/deleted. Not gated with a pro-followup run by default (see run_pilot.py cmd_readonly_desktop).

tasks.instruction: Open Calculator. Using ONLY mouse clicks on the on-screen buttons (no keyboard), compute (37 x 4) - (128 / 8) + 15^2, reporting the running result after each operation. Do not use keyboard shortcuts.
tasks.predicted_failure: Long click chain with no formula parser to fall back on; operator precedence must be sequenced manually, so an early misclick compounds into a wrong final answer with no self-check.

tasks.instruction: In System Settings, navigate to the Accessibility > Display pane, toggle 'Reduce Transparency' on, confirm visually that it took effect, then toggle it back off so the setting ends exactly as it started.
tasks.predicted_failure: Several similarly-named neighboring toggles (Reduce Motion, Increase Contrast) invite a wrong-target click; task also requires ending in the *original* state, not just any state.
tasks.file_risk: none — system preference, explicitly reverted at the end

tasks.instruction: Open Activity Monitor, sort the process list by Memory descending, filter to 'My Processes', open the top process's info window, and report its thread count. Do not quit or force-close any process.
tasks.predicted_failure: Nested filter dropdown + separate inspector window; a silent click failure on either would surface as a wrong or stale reading rather than an obvious error.

tasks.instruction: In Preview, open an image, zoom to exactly 150% using the toolbar zoom control, use the marquee selection tool (not any annotation/markup tool) to select a region, read the pixel dimensions from the live selection readout, then reset zoom to 100% and close the window without saving.
tasks.predicted_failure: Selection readout updates dynamically while dragging, and hitting an exact zoom percentage via toolbar clicks is a precise-targeting gesture class distinct from a simple click.
tasks.file_risk: none — marquee tool only, explicitly closes unsaved

pool.source: WebArena (CMS/admin transactional flows, GPT-4 14.4% overall success)
pool.instruction: In a WordPress demo admin panel, create a new post, insert a featured image via the media-library modal, and save as draft (do not publish).
pool.predicted_failure: Modal-over-modal (media library opens inside post editor) causes silent click failures analogous to the Photoshop nested-menu bug.

pool.instruction: On Reddit, navigate to a specific subreddit, apply a sort filter, open a specific post matching a description, and save it (no posting/commenting).
pool.predicted_failure: Early misstep (wrong filter or wrong post) compounds without a self-check before the agent declares success.

pool.instruction: On a smaller/niche e-commerce site (non-Amazon) with a non-standard variant selector, add a specific size+color configuration to cart.
pool.predicted_failure: Layout doesn't match common patterns the model has seen most; wrong variant selected or state lost across steps.

pool.instruction: On Google Flights, search a specific multi-city itinerary using the date-picker and multi-city mode.
pool.predicted_failure: Date-picker widget interaction (open/close/select range) is a known-hard gesture class; possible easier control given WebVoyager's higher baseline success rates.

pool.instruction: In GIMP, crop an open image, rename the layer, and export as PNG.
pool.predicted_failure: Direct desktop analogue of the Photoshop-web bug; tests whether nested-menu coordinate drift is web-specific or general.

pool.instruction: In LibreOffice Calc, create a second sheet, enter data, write a cross-sheet formula referencing it, and apply cell formatting.
pool.predicted_failure: Same logical task as the Sheets pool item below (wa-sheets) but desktop-native — controlled pair to isolate web-vs-desktop effects on the same task.

pool.instruction: In the desktop file manager, rename a file via the nested right-click context menu, then move it into a new folder.
pool.predicted_failure: Most direct desktop analogue of the original confirmed bug (rename via nested menu).

pool.instruction: In VS Code, edit a specific line in an open file, save it, then run a command in the integrated terminal and confirm its output.
pool.predicted_failure: Mixes typing/keyboard shortcuts with GUI; long horizon with a verification step at the end.

pool.source: Direct replication of findings.md session d000f506
pool.instruction: In a Figma file, rename a layer via the right-click context menu.
pool.predicted_failure: Same click-doesn't-register / coordinate drift on canvas-rendered UI as Photoshop Web.

pool.instruction: In Excalidraw, move a shape, resize it, then multi-select and group it with a second shape.
pool.predicted_failure: Canvas drag + multi-select, no DOM semantics at all; tests whether drag gesture handling (not just click) also fails.

pool.instruction: In Google Docs, rename the document via the editable title text.
pool.predicted_failure: Expected easier than Figma/Photoshop — semantic DOM, not canvas. Used to isolate whether canvas-vs-DOM is really the hardness variable.

pool.instruction: In Google Sheets, create a new tab, enter data across multiple cells, write a formula referencing the other tab, and apply cell formatting — 15+ atomic actions.
pool.predicted_failure: Early slip compounds; no self-check before declaring done. Paired with osworld-libreoffice for web-vs-desktop comparison.

pool.instruction: On Trello, drag a card from one list to another.
pool.predicted_failure: Agent attempts a click instead of a mousedown-move-up drag gesture sequence.

pool.source: Adversarial construction (untested territory per findings.md contrast table)
pool.instruction: Browse a site anonymously (no login) and proceed as if buying/saving, hitting a real login wall only at checkout/save (~step 10 of 15), not at step 1.
pool.predicted_failure: Tests whether honest 'blocked' self-reporting still fires after real progress/investment, unlike the existing clean day-one blocker examples.
